author: MikeEli
post:
$POET $NVDA 
1. Rubin Ultra will use CPO instead of pluggables which is slated for 2027. 
2. LITEON POET announcement  in March for CPO (prototypes in late 2026, production in 2027 for CPO) matches the Rubin Ultra production window perfectly.  
3.POET-LITEON partnership (announced March 16, 2026) is the only one targeting the specific "unsolved" problem of the Rubin Ultra platform: Low-cost, high-yield assembly of CPO (Co-Packaged Optics).  
4. Poet's product Blazar, is intended for CPO and their CEO stated that they expect new customer engagements this year revolving around that product. 
5. Liteon is already tied to Nividia for their cooling tech among other products. 
6. None of Nividia existing partners solve the "assembly bottleneck"—the manual, expensive process of attaching fibers to chips.   
​POET’s Optical Interposer and Smart FAU are designed for automated, semiconductor-style assembly. If NVIDIA wants to build millions of Rubin Ultra units, they cannot rely on manual fiber alignment

author: topstockalerts
post:
Google announced Gemma 4, a family of open-source AI models released under an Apache 2.0 license. The company said developers have downloaded Gemma models over 400 million times since the first release, creating more than 100,000 variants. 
Gemma 4 includes four model sizes: Effective 2B, Effective 4B, 26B Mixture of Experts, and 31B Dense. The 31B model ranks third globally on the Arena AI text leaderboard among open models, while the 26B ranks sixth. Google noted the models are built on the same research and technology as Gemini 3. 
The models support advanced reasoning, agentic workflows with function calling and structured JSON output, code generation, and native multimodal processing (video, image, audio). Edge models offer a 128K context window, while larger models reach up to 256K, and all are trained on 140+ languages. 
The unquantized bfloat16 weights of the 26B and 31B models fit on a single NVIDIA H100 (80GB GPU).

author: DonCorleone77
post:
$AAPL $MSFT $NVDA $AMZN $NFLX  
 
I made the following post on my subscription site and thought I would post it here in case it could help others: 
 
HOW ELITE TRADERS MAKE BIG MONEY 
 
So you all know by now that I study elite traders to determine what they do to make big money. 
Along those lines I have shared with you a quote attributed to Jesse Livermore that says: 
 
"It never was my thinking that made the big money for me. It always was my sitting. Got that? My sitting tight!" 
 
So what was Livermore talking about here? 
He was basically talking about two things: 
 
1) Having the technical mastery to be able to identify big structural moves as they are happening; and 
2) Having the emotional mastery to ride those structural moves to make the big money. 
 
In fact, the quote attributed to Livermore also added: 
 
"I've known many men who were right at exactly the right time, and began buying or selling stocks when prices were at the very level which should show the greatest profit. And their experience invariably matched mine--that is, they made no real money out of it. Men who can both be right and sit tight are uncommon.” 
 
Along those lines, I wanted to share with you some data that helps expound on this concept. 
 
On April 9th of 2025, Donald Trump paused his previously imposed tariffs. 
That move sent stocks soaring as if they had been tightly coiled for a massive launch higher. 
If you identified that MACRO opportunity then you knew the line of least resistance in the overall market was higher and you would have gotten aggressive with your portfolio. 
 
Now...getting the macro picture right is critical to making big gains. 
However, to make the really big money you also need to identify the correct SECTORS and top STOCKS within those sectors. 
Then, you also needed to have the emotional mastery to be able to HOLD those stocks through the ups and downs of the structural move that follows. 
As a swing trader, this is EXACTLY what I spend most of my time attempting to do. To recap: 
 
1) Identify the line of least resistance in the overall market; 
2) Identify asymmetrical risk and inflection points within sectors; 
3) Identify the leading stocks within those sectors that may participate in a big structural move; and 
4) Have the patience to manage the trade to benefit from the move that follows. 
 
To be clear, developing BOTH the technical mastery to identify these trends AND the emotional mastery to participate in the big part of the moves is VERY uncommon. 
In my estimation, it is these traits that help make up the 5% of elite traders that make big money in the markets. 
The 5% of elite traders are also the ones that don't chase tons of stocks in different markets that do not offer the ideal set-up that they look for before getting aggressive. 
Again, finding someone with BOTH the technical mastery and the patience/discipline not to over-trade is uncommon. 
 
With all that in mind, I wanted to share a list of stocks that I follow that could have offered massive gains over the last year if you identified the correct sectors and individual stocks within those sectors that were ripe for a structural move. 
 
PLEASE NOTE THAT I AM NOT SUGGESTING I BOUGHT ALL OF THESE STOCKS. 
In fact, I did buy some of them but I missed A LOT of them.

The world faces two scenarios: either countries accept that Iran is effectively charging tolls and establishing controlled corridors, or they try to take action. But even with action, shipping remains under constant threat. In practice, this likely means the Strait of Hormuz will be written off as a safe commercial route: only friendly nations will be able to use it. Europe and other oil-dependent regions will need to find alternative routes and energy sources.
In short: this is not a temporary problem that a war can solve. The threat is structural, and the world must adapt to a reality in which the Hormuz chokepoint is no longer freely acces

author: Utreggg
post:
$ASML $NVDA $BTC.X $ETH.X $SOL.X 

Why Crypto Works Differently
Crypto works differently from traditional markets. These are not companies, but networks driven by sentiment, expectations, and capital flows. Everything revolves around momentum.
In an uptrend, “buy the dip” works. But in a downtrend, you often do the opposite: you keep buying and end up adding more downside. That’s not a strategy — that’s hope.
Let’s be honest: you’re not steadily building wealth here. You’re moving with momentum. And right now, that momentum simply isn’t there. We’re in a phase of downward pressure, uncertainty, and doubt.
This is not the time to be fully exposed to crypto. Focus on assets with stronger fundamentals, like ETFs, and build steadily there.
In crypto, you don’t win by always being in the market, but by moving with momentum — and sometimes that means staying on the sidelines.
